rulings.Case Title: DAVID ABRAMOVITZ ET AL VS. FARHAT BAJJALIYA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Anton Andryeyev'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Supplemental Responses To Requests For Admissions And Form Interrogatories, And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, October 25, 2018, Line 1, THIRD PARTY ANTON ANDRYEYEV'S Motion To Compel Supplemental Responses To Requests For Admissions And Form Interrogatories, And For Sanctions Judge Van Aken will hear this motion in department 506 at 10:30 a.m. Plaintiff Anton Andryeyev's Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Supplemental Responses To Requests For Admissions And Form Interrogatories, And For Sanctions is GRANTED. Andryeyev's Requests for Admissions (Set 1) and Form Interrogatories (Set 2) were proper. Defendants objected to the RFAs on the grounds that the RFAs were premature and not fully complete in themselves. These objections are overruled. Responding parties must answer discovery fully on the basis of facts presently known to them. Nor did the requests violate the rule that a request must be full and complete in and of itself. "[R]eference to other materials or documents or incorporation by reference of such materials is prohibited where the effect is to undermine the rule of 35." (Clement v. Alegre (2009) 177 Cal.App.4th 1277, 1290.) Where the request incorporates other material but does not undermine the discovery limits, the request is proper and "[t]o conclude otherwise in these circumstances would require a cumbersome redrafting of questions and potentially multiple rounds of discovery, undermining the [Discovery] Act's aim of clarity and simplicity and returning to the era of papering to death the opposition." (Id. [internal quotation marks and brackets omitted].) Defendants objected in their opposition papers to the RFAs on the grounds of work product but that objection was not asserted in the original responses and in any event Defendants may properly be asked to admit or deny facts without invading attorney work product. With respect to the Form Interrogatories, Defendants' objections to FI 17.1 are based on its objections to the RFAs; those objections are overruled. Defendants shall provide code-compliant responses and verifications to RFAs Set 1 and FIs Set 2 within 10 days of the date notice of entry of this order is served on Defendants. Defendants' objections were without substantial justification. Defendants shall pay sanctions in the amount of $3000 to Plaintiffs' counsel. Defendants and their counsel are jointly and severally liable. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: DARREN MANFRO VS. DANYI YU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Plaintiff Responses To Defendants Discovery Requests; Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, October 25, 2018, Line 6, DEFENDANTS DANYI YU, CHESTER LIN'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff Responses To Defendants Discovery Requests; Request For Monetary Sanctions Judge Van Aken will hear this motion in department 506 at 10:30 a.m. Defendants Danyi Yu and Chester Lin's motion to compel Plaintiff's Responses To Defendants' Discovery Requests and Request For Monetary Sanctions is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Defendants have failed to comply with CRC 3.1345 requiring a Separate Statement in support of a motion seeking further discovery responses and the Court is unable to determine from the face of Defendants' moving papers whether or not Plaintiff's responses to date comply with his obligations under the Code. Nonetheless Plaintiff's opposition to the motion to compel concedes that further supplement to Plaintiff's discovery responses is necessary and forthcoming. Plaintiff shall provide supplemental responses to form interrogatories set 1, special interrogatories set 1, and requests for production of documents set 1, on or before November 28, 2018. The request for monetary sanctions is denied because the circumstances of Plaintiff's incarceration make the imposition of sanctions unjust. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: ALIGN TECHNOLOGY, INC. VS. TIMOTHY DUANE HUBBS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION - Notice Of Motion And Motion For Assignment Of Rights, Restraining Order And Turnover Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, October 25, 2018, Line 7, PLAINTIFF ALIGN TECHNOLOGY, INC.'S Motion For Assignment Of Rights, Restraining Order And Turnover Order Judge Van Aken will hear this motion in department 506 at 10:30 a.m. Judgment creditor Align Technology Inc.'s Motion For Assignment Of Rights, Restraining Order And Turnover Order is DENIED. With regard to the motion for assignment of rights, C.C.P. section 708.510(d) provides that a right to payment may be assigned "only to the extent necessary to satisfy the money judgment." Judgment creditor has provided no evidence that its broad proposed assignment is only to the extent necessary to satisfy the money judgment. Moreover section 708.510(c) directs courts to consider specified factors in deciding whether to order assignment; judgment creditor has provided no information about any factor other than section 708.510(c)(3). Further, judgment creditor seeks assignment of deposit accounts; it is unclear from the statute whether rights to deposit accounts are assignable here. With regard to the motion for turnover order pursuant to C.C.P. section 669.0940(a), that statute applies only "[i]f a writ of execution is issued." With regard to the request for restraining order under section 708.520(a), the purpose of such an order is to preserve for the judgment creditor the benefit of an assignment made under section 708.510, and the judgment creditor has not made a meritorious request for an assignment under section 708.510. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: JACKELINE ESCOBAR ET AL VS. MAX'S SF OPERA CAFE, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 25, 2018, Line 9, DEFENDANTs MAX'S SF OPERA CAFE, INC., MAX'S OPERA CAFE, INC., MAX'S OPERA INC., 601 MAX'S INC., MAX'S OPERA CAFE, DENNIS BERKOWIYZ, MAHMOOD AZAD, ALI AZAD and MEHRDAD HEJAZIAN's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendants' demurrer to all six causes of action in the first amended complaint is overruled as to all six causes of action. Plaintiffs' PAGA notice lists the specific provisions of the Labor Code that defendants allegedly violated and includes facts and theories to support the alleged violations, thereby satisfying the requirements of Labor Code section 2699.3(a)(1)(A). Liberally construing the complaint per CCP section 452, plaintiffs alleged sufficient ultimate facts to support all of their claims. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: KENLEY GAFFKE ET AL VS. SAN REMO HOTEL, LP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Defendants Petition To Compel Binding Arbitration Regarding Plaintiff Kenley Gaffke
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 25, 2018, Line 10, DEFENDANTS SAN REMO HOTEL, LP and ROBERT FIELD's Notice Of Hearing On Defendants Petition To Compel Binding Arbitration Regarding Plaintiff Kenley Gaffke Defendants San Remo Hotel, LP and Robert Field's petition to compel arbitration of the claims alleged by plaintiff Kenly Gaffke is granted. Moving defendants have shown that the parties entered in a valid enforceable arbitration agreement covering Mr. Gaffke's claims. Although the agreement was not signed by the moving defendants, their conduct showed they considered themselves bound by the agreement. (Serafin v. Balco Properties Ltd., LLC (2015) 235 Cal.App.4th 165, 177.) The identity of the parties bound by the agreement can be ascertained by extrinsic evidence and, per Mr. Gaffke's declaration, the intent of the agreement was to bind San Remo Hotel, LP and its owners, among others. Although the arbitration agreement has indicia of procedural unconscionability because it is a contract of adhesion, Mr. Gaffke has failed to show any substantive unconscionability. The discovery provided in Civil Code section 1283.05 protects Mr. Gaffke's ability to vindicate his claims and the agreement impliedly obligates moving defendants to pay for the cost of arbitration, an obligation acknowledged by their counsel. Mr. Gaffke's claims must be resolved by binding arbitration and all proceedings regarding those claims in this case are stayed pending the conclusion of the arbitration proceedings. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for moving defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk
